description: The Di Lauro clan is an Italian crime clan and part of the Camorra in Naples, founded in 1982 by Paolo Di Lauro. It operates in several neighborhoods of Naples and surrounding municipalities. At its peak in the late 1990s and early 2000s, the clan earned over €500,000 daily from drug sales, making Secondigliano the largest open-air drug market in Europe. Despite arrests, the clan has adapted with a business-oriented model, focusing on international smuggling and counterfeiting.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Di Lauro clan funneled the money into real estate, buying dozens of flats in Naples, shops in France and the Netherlands... Cosimo Di Lauro sometimes travelled to Paris to control the business of the clan in France.
country_links.context: The Di Lauro clan bought shops in France and Cosimo Di Lauro traveled to Paris to control the clan's business there.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Di Lauro clan funneled the money into real estate, buying dozens of flats in Naples, shops in France and the Netherlands... The clan also produces counterfeit clothes in the Netherlands.
country_links.context: The Di Lauro clan bought shops in the Netherlands and produces counterfeit clothes there.

country_links.evidence_quote: in October 2021, the Spanish police arrested a suspected member of the organization in Málaga, who was in charge of the tobacco smuggling activities for the organization in Spain.
country_links.context: A suspected member of the Di Lauro clan was arrested in Málaga, who was in charge of tobacco smuggling activities for the organization in Spain.
